# Plan: Add `mistral-instruct-v3` Specialized Chat Template Handler

## Context

llama.cpp's Jinja-based chat pipeline routes recognized template sources through
`common_chat_try_specialized_template()` (in `common/chat.cpp`) before falling
back to the autoparser.  The Ministral 3 handler already covers Mistral-family
templates that carry `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` markers.  Mistral Instruct v0.3 — the
classic `[INST]/[/INST]` + `</s>` format with system messages prepended inline
to the first user turn — has no `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` and needs its own handler so
it gets a deterministic PEG-based parser instead of the autoparser lottery.

**Constraint:** add new code only; do not touch any existing handler function or
`src/llama-chat.h`.

---

## Only File to Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `common/chat.cpp` | Add one static handler function + one detection block |

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Add the handler function

Insert the following static function **immediately before**
`common_chat_params_init_gigachat_v3` (line 1446) — any position before
`common_chat_try_specialized_template` works; placing it with the other handlers
keeps the file tidy.

```cpp
// Mistral Instruct v0.3 - classic [INST]/[/INST] format
// See: https://huggingface.co/mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3
// Format:
//   [INST] {user} [/INST]{assistant}</s>
//   System message is prepended to the first user turn, separated by \n\n.
static common_chat_params common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(
        const common_chat_template &          tmpl,
        const autoparser::generation_params & inputs) {
    common_chat_params data;

    data.prompt = common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl(tmpl, inputs);
    data.format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE;

    auto parser = build_chat_peg_parser([&](common_chat_peg_builder & p) {
        return p.literal(inputs.generation_prompt) + p.content(p.rest());
    });

    data.parser = parser.save();

    return data;
}
```

**Notes on design choices:**
- `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl` renders the Jinja template — it
  handles the system-into-first-user-turn prepending natively via the template.
- `COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE` + a plain `content(rest())` parser is the
  correct approach for a content-only (no-tool) format; identical to the
  content-only branch of `common_chat_params_init_gigachat_v3`.
- `p.literal(inputs.generation_prompt)` (not `p.prefix`) is used because there
  is no optional reasoning/thinking prefix — same pattern used in
  `common_chat_params_init_functionary_v3_2` and `gigachat_v3`.
- No `preserved_tokens`, `grammar`, or thinking fields needed for this format.

### Step 2 — Register the detection in `common_chat_try_specialized_template`

Add the new detection block **at the end** of `common_chat_try_specialized_template`,
immediately before the `return std::nullopt;` line (currently line 1690).

```cpp
    // Mistral Instruct v0.3 - [INST]/[/INST] format without [SYSTEM_PROMPT].
    // The absence of [SYSTEM_PROMPT] is what distinguishes this from the
    // Ministral/Magistral Large 3 handler above.
    if (src.find("[INST]")         != std::string::npos &&
        src.find("[/INST]")        != std::string::npos &&
        src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
        LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v0.3\n");
        return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
    }
```

**Why this detection is correct:**
- Ministral 3 detection (line 1640) checks `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` **present** — so
  it fires first and is never confused with v0.3.
- No other currently-registered handler matches `[INST]`/`[/INST]`-carrying
  Jinja templates, so ordering within the function is safe.
- Templates like Mistral Nemo (`mistralai-Mistral-Nemo-Instruct-2407.jinja`)
  also have `[INST]` without `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`, but they have no specialized
  handler today; routing them through this handler (content-only PEG) is
  safe and correct for plain chat use — tool-call support for those templates
  remains a separate future concern.

---

## Critical Lines / Anchor Points

| What | File | Approx. line |
|------|------|-------------|
| Insert handler before | `common/chat.cpp` | 1446 (`common_chat_params_init_gigachat_v3`) |
| Insert detection before | `common/chat.cpp` | 1690 (`return std::nullopt;`) |
| Existing Ministral 3 check (reference) | `common/chat.cpp` | 1640 |

---

## Key Reused Utilities

| Utility | File | Purpose |
|---------|------|---------|
| `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl` | `common/chat.cpp` ~line 820 | Renders Jinja template to string |
| `build_chat_peg_parser` | `common/chat.cpp` | Builds PEG parser |
| `common_chat_peg_builder::literal` | via builder | Matches generation prompt exactly |
| `common_chat_peg_builder::content` + `rest` | via builder | Captures remainder as model output |

---

## Verification

1. **Manual smoke test with existing Jinja template:**
   ```bash
   ./llama-cli --model mistral-7b-instruct-v0.3.gguf \
               --chat-template-file /path/to/mistral_v03.jinja \
               --log-level debug \
               -p "Hello"
   ```
   The debug log should print:
   `Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v0.3`

2. **Negative test — Ministral 3 must not be affected:**
   Run the same with `mistralai-Ministral-3-14B-Reasoning-2512.jinja`; log must
   still print `Using specialized template: Ministral/Magistral Large 3`.

3. **Unit test hook (if test infra is exercised):**
   `tests/test-chat-template.cpp` already covers Mistral v1/v3/v7; a new case
   using a minimal Mistral v0.3 Jinja source (contains `[INST]`, `[/INST]`,
   no `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`) would confirm detection and prompt formatting.

4. **Build check:**
   ```bash
   cmake --build build --target common
   ```
   No compile errors expected — only additive changes.

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `mistral-instruct-v3` Specialized Chat Template Handler

## Context

llama.cpp's Jinja-based chat pipeline routes recognized template sources through
`common_chat_try_specialized_template()` (in `common/chat.cpp`) before falling
back to the autoparser.  The Ministral 3 handler already covers Mistral-family
templates that carry `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` markers.  Mistral Instruct v0.3 — the
classic `[INST]/[/INST]` + `</s>` format with system messages prepended inline
to the first user turn — has no `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` and needs its own handler so
it gets a deterministic PEG-based parser instead of the autoparser lottery.

**Constraint:** add new code only; do not touch any existing handler function or
`src/llama-chat.h`.

---

## Only File to Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `common/chat.cpp` | Add one static handler function + one detection block |

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Add the handler function

Insert the following static function **immediately before**
`common_chat_params_init_gigachat_v3` (line 1446) — any position before
`common_chat_try_specialized_template` works; placing it with the other handlers
keeps the file tidy.

```cpp
// Mistral Instruct v0.3 - classic [INST]/[/INST] format
// See: https://huggingface.co/mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3
// Format:
//   [INST] {user} [/INST]{assistant}</s>
//   System message is prepended to the first user turn, separated by \n\n.
static common_chat_params common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(
        const common_chat_template &          tmpl,
        const autoparser::generation_params & inputs) {
    common_chat_params data;

    data.prompt = common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl(tmpl, inputs);
    data.format = COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE;

    auto parser = build_chat_peg_parser([&](common_chat_peg_builder & p) {
        return p.literal(inputs.generation_prompt) + p.content(p.rest());
    });

    data.parser = parser.save();

    return data;
}
```

**Notes on design choices:**
- `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl` renders the Jinja template — it
  handles the system-into-first-user-turn prepending natively via the template.
- `COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE` + a plain `content(rest())` parser is the
  correct approach for a content-only (no-tool) format; identical to the
  content-only branch of `common_chat_params_init_gigachat_v3`.
- `p.literal(inputs.generation_prompt)` (not `p.prefix`) is used because there
  is no optional reasoning/thinking prefix — same pattern used in
  `common_chat_params_init_functionary_v3_2` and `gigachat_v3`.
- No `preserved_tokens`, `grammar`, or thinking fields needed for this format.

### Step 2 — Register the detection in `common_chat_try_specialized_template`

Add the new detection block **at the end** of `common_chat_try_specialized_template`,
immediately before the `return std::nullopt;` line (currently line 1690).

```cpp
    // Mistral Instruct v0.3 - [INST]/[/INST] format without [SYSTEM_PROMPT].
    // The absence of [SYSTEM_PROMPT] is what distinguishes this from the
    // Ministral/Magistral Large 3 handler above.
    if (src.find("[INST]")         != std::string::npos &&
        src.find("[/INST]")        != std::string::npos &&
        src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
        LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v0.3\n");
        return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
    }
```

**Why this detection is correct:**
- Ministral 3 detection (line 1640) checks `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` **present** — so
  it fires first and is never confused with v0.3.
- No other currently-registered handler matches `[INST]`/`[/INST]`-carrying
  Jinja templates, so ordering within the function is safe.
- Templates like Mistral Nemo (`mistralai-Mistral-Nemo-Instruct-2407.jinja`)
  also have `[INST]` without `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`, but they have no specialized
  handler today; routing them through this handler (content-only PEG) is
  safe and correct for plain chat use — tool-call support for those templates
  remains a separate future concern.
